tweet.text:
Wix at 1x ARR is not a SaaS bloodbath. 

It is a reckoning for businesses that were always just sales and marketing operations dressed up as software.

1. The client-server to SaaS shift was more disruptive than anything AI is doing to software right now. This is incremental. The market is finally pricing it that way.

2. SaaS was never that hard to spin up. When the moat is a sales team and a logo, the multiple compresses. That is not a surprise, that is gravity.

3. The companies that survive this are the ones with complex customer relationships and data sets that cannot be recreated. Wix does not have that. Base44 might not either.

4. Vibe coding tools doing $170M ARR getting priced at $2.1BN is the same mistake in reverse. Getting real workflow change adopted at scale takes longer than the ARR curve suggests.

5. The best software companies right now are not pure software companies. The ones with physical world integration and proprietary data loops are harder to replicate and har

AI/ML.text:
Galaxy Research: Inference Capital Markets
> A new onchain financial layer is emerging around AI inference, using crypto to tokenize compute access, finance GPU infrastructure, and create markets for AI services.
> For now, inference capital markets, both onchain and off, are tiny relative to the growth of the AI industry. For onchain offerings to scale, they have to prove the advantage they bring is sustainable.
Source:
https://www.galaxy.com/insights/research/inference-capital-markets-ai-compute-gpu-futures-onchain-crypto

tweet.text:
Today we're launching http://gpuspec.io.

Buying or selling GPU capacity means describing the same cluster over and over: model, count, region, fabric, term, price. It's slow to write, easy to garble, and impossible to track once it's been forwarded a few times.

the new interface is up at

fun.noxa.eth

you can access it directly via brave built-in ens support

or via services like eth limo/link

https://fun.noxa.eth.limo
https://fun.noxa.eth.link

the interface lets you browse a historical snapshot of the tokens launched on noxa in the past, and claim any creator fees, as well as seeing the tokens you launched or where you are the fee receiver

we have pondered for long on the matters of token vamps, and the new token launch spam that has been going on

if crypto as a space has to move forward and improve, the change needs to come from the people

the biggest coins currently present on robinhood, the staples that made the chain what it has become today, even if still in its infancy

people loved the cat, it has been liberated

the legacy of the degens will stay forever

there is only one solution to avoid dilution of the tokens, the staples

and that is to keep new launches disabled

the noxa infrastructure was also never built to sustain suc

Ryan Watkins: Crypto Is Entering Its Biggest Adoption Phase
> Ryan Watkins argues blockchains are becoming the global API for money and finance, set to power stablecoins, tokenized equities, perps, prediction markets, onchain credit, and more.
> Bringing crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents, with effort to come from both crypto-native apps (e.g Phantom and Fomo) and incumbents like Robinhood and Stripe.
> “Time to look past any month-to-month volatility and focus on the bigger picture unfolding over coming quarters and years…Time to lock in.”
Source:
https://x.com/RyanWatkins_/status/2075595100789318068

tweet.text:
Blockchains are a global API for money and finance.

We’re going to plug this API into every app where it makes sense over the coming years and bring crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents.

Stablecoin payments + savings, tokenized assets, onchain credit markets, perps for everything, prediction markets… the list goes on.

All these products are composable, so builders will only be limited by their imaginations on what this could all look like.

Some efforts will be homegrown like Phantom and Fomo.

Others will be established players like Robinhood and Stripe plugging it in and championing this technology.

In any case we will win.

Prediction Markets.text:
overview of the thesis and investments made by theory ventures
https://x.com/ttunguz/status/2075629199252144550

tweet.text:
Three years ago, we launched Theory Ventures with a simple premise : AI would reshape how software is built, sold, deployed, & operated. Within that world, we would build a concentrated, thesis-driven firm.

The market moved faster than even the most bullish expectations after the ChatGPT moment. Frontier models leapt from delicate demos to production systems. Open source models have become substitutes for enterprise workloads. Inference emerged as the dominant market in AI.

Underpinning all of this, AI compresses time. New models are released every 41 days. Companies reach $100m in revenue in record time. We all achieve more faster.

In celebration of our anniversary, we wanted to trace that mechanism through the market shifts of the last three years.

The first casualty of compressed time is the old language of venture capital. Seed, Series A, Series B categories still exist, but they describe the financial product companies seek rather than rather than company maturity. Venture fir

tweet.text:
Announcing ComputeConnect, the financial industry’s first exchange-for-physical (EFP) network for compute, coming soon from Architect and @ComputeDesk. ComputeConnect links US exchange-traded compute futures to compute capacity delivery.

Exchange-listed cash-settled compute futures are entering US markets to correct course on the current AI economy, reorienting debt to long-term growth:

 • Creating price discovery and transparency independent of any single capacity provider.
 • Establishing a forward curve for measuring deprecation and forecasting supply and demand.
 • Providing financial hedges for compute consumers and producers.
 • Enabling hedge funds, ETF companies, and traders to gain long and short financial exposure to compute.

US cash-settled compute futures lack a physical delivery mechanism, and ComputeConnect fills this gap.

Existing physically settled futures such as energy and agriculturals require their clearing house (DCO) to set a uniform standard for the grade and

tweet.text:
Introducing Receive Policies

On most blockchains, any token can be sent to any account: There's no easy way to restrict who can send you assets or refuse an unwanted token.

Receive policies bring these features to Tempo, enforced by the protocol.

tweet.text:
Wix at 1x ARR is not a SaaS bloodbath. 

It is a reckoning for businesses that were always just sales and marketing operations dressed up as software.

1. The client-server to SaaS shift was more disruptive than anything AI is doing to software right now. This is incremental. The market is finally pricing it that way.

2. SaaS was never that hard to spin up. When the moat is a sales team and a logo, the multiple compresses. That is not a surprise, that is gravity.

3. The companies that survive this are the ones with complex customer relationships and data sets that cannot be recreated. Wix does not have that. Base44 might not either.

4. Vibe coding tools doing $170M ARR getting priced at $2.1BN is the same mistake in reverse. Getting real workflow change adopted at scale takes longer than the ARR curve suggests.

5. The best software companies right now are not pure software companies. The ones with physical world integration and proprietary data loops are harder to replicate and har

Robotics.text:
data refineries: turning abundant data into trustworthy, actionable intelligence
this is the moat
https://x.com/michellelsun/status/2076705184898785637

tweet.text:
Data volume is no longer the bottleneck for robotics training. 

It’s building dynamic pipelines - like oil refineries in the AI age - that guarantee trustworthy lineage + usable signal for training and deployment.

A single robot spits out terabytes of raw camera, LiDAR & IMU streams daily. 

Training data supply is exploding globally, especially with the surge of data collection startups across India, China, and beyond.

In order for these data to be useful, we need robust processing pipelines that handle : 
- Provenance tracking (where did this frame come from? which embodiment? under what conditions?)
- Cleaning, synchronization, labeling
- Repeatedly & reliably turning it into high-quality multi-modal tokens for training and real-time deployment

The new moat in robotics is the robust, dynamic data infrastructure that turns raw observations into trustworthy, actionable intelligence.

Who’s building the best data refineries?

I think there is general confusion around how AI works, AI tokenomics, and ultimately *what is actually priced in* for the AI trade - and that some of the existing arguments are at odds with one another

Firstly to clear this up - what Brad and Gavin are saying are completely in agreement, what Gavin is laying out here is the *mega bull case* as he so states in the first sentence of his tweet lol

The base case we are all living with is that the labs are going to continue to generate a significant amount of revenue this year and next year. OpenAI was already the fastest growing company of all time (and still is)... but Anthropic has just grown *SO* fast that OpenAI's growth look slow by comparison

The basic chain for all of this together is as follows:

Power (generation, interconnect, regulation) ->
DC Shell (construction, equipment, regulation) -> 
Semiconductors (compute, memory, interconnect, adv packaging, wafer capacity) ->
Hardware (networking, storage) ->
Software (data, infra

The Physical AI data space is exploding, but most vendors still share samples through Google Drive or Hugging Face links that are slow and painful to evaluate.

We built the Human Archive Catalog so researchers can quickly browse and filter our off-the-shelf datasets by environment, hardware configuration, modality, annotations, and more.

If you’re a robotics or world model researcher, request access below:

https://catalog.humanarchive-qa.ai/request-access

Morgan Stanley: Humanoids

Shifts in BoM Cost Composition (2026e–2040e)

> Rising Chips/Compute Cost: The share of BoM cost for Chips is projected to triple, rising sharply from 5% in 2026e to 15% by 2040e.

> Sensors (Force/Tactile): Remain the largest single component cost initially at 24% in 2026e, but steadily decline to 17% by 2040e.

> Motors: Hold a relatively stable but slightly declining share, moving from 22% down to 19%.

> Reducers: Drop slightly from 15% to 12%.

Projected Demand & Market Growth

> Motors: Projected to reach a massive US$2,073bn in market size, representing a 170x increase.

> Reducers: Expected to reach US$1,370bn (a 157x increase).

> Edge Compute: Reaches US$1,233bn, driven by the single highest multiplier growth of 1,904x.

> Bearings: Set for a staggering 370x growth to a value of US$316bn.

> Cameras & LiDAR: Cameras scale by 84x (US$268bn), while LiDAR scales by 40x (US$128bn).

overview of the thesis and investments made by theory ventures
https://x.com/ttunguz/status/2075629199252144550

tweet.text:
Three years ago, we launched Theory Ventures with a simple premise : AI would reshape how software is built, sold, deployed, & operated. Within that world, we would build a concentrated, thesis-driven firm.

The market moved faster than even the most bullish expectations after the ChatGPT moment. Frontier models leapt from delicate demos to production systems. Open source models have become substitutes for enterprise workloads. Inference emerged as the dominant market in AI.

Underpinning all of this, AI compresses time. New models are released every 41 days. Companies reach $100m in revenue in record time. We all achieve more faster.

In celebration of our anniversary, we wanted to trace that mechanism through the market shifts of the last three years.

The first casualty of compressed time is the old language of venture capital. Seed, Series A, Series B categories still exist, but they describe the financial product companies seek rather than rather than company maturity. Venture fir
